title: Add data migration script for email normalization
description: Normalizes all user email addresses to lowercase with a migration script.
diff:
diff --git a/migrations/normalize_emails.py b/migrations/normalize_emails.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b6c7d8e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/migrations/normalize_emails.py
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+from database import get_session
+
+def migrate():
+    session = get_session()
+    users = session.execute("SELECT id, email FROM users").fetchall()
+    for user in users:
+        normalized = user.email.lower().strip()
+        if len(normalized) > 100:
+            normalized = normalized[:100]  # Silent truncation!
+        session.execute(
+            "UPDATE users SET email = %s WHERE id = %s",
+            (normalized, user.id)
+        )
+    session.commit()
comments: rachel: Silent truncation! Emails longer than 100 chars get truncated without any warning. This corrupts data — a user with 'very.long.email@subdomain.example.com' (let's say 101 chars) gets their email silently mangled, breaking their login.
sam: Also no batch processing — loading all users into memory will OOM on large tables. Use pagination.
author-tara: I'll add length validation and batch processing.
author: tara
